I INTRODUCTION 
 
I.1 Product name  
ANTARCTIC_OMI_SI_extent 
 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
Sea ice extent is defined here as the measurement of the area of the ocean covered at least by 
15% of sea ice. Monthly mean (1993-2014) seasonal cycle time series of total area for sea ice 
extent are calculated for the southern hemisphere, e.g. Antarctica. Total area annual mean time 
series 1993 -2023 for sea ice extent are calculated for the same domain. Sea Ice Extent is 
expressed here in millions of km². 
I.3 CMEMS products used as input for the computations 
 
Product name (and Type) Documentation 
GLOBAL_MULTIYEAR_PHY_ENS_00
1_031 
QUID : CMEMS-GLO-QUID-001-031.pdf  
PUM:CMEMS-GLO-PUM-001-031.pdf 
(copernicus.eu)  
 
 
 CMEMS Quality Information Document for 
Antarctica Sea Ice Extent 
Issue : 3.0 
29.09.2024 
 
 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
5 
II SCIENTIFIC VALIDATION 
More details and full scientific evaluation can be found in the CMEMS Ocean State Report 
(Samuelsen et al., 2016; Samuelsen et al., 2018).  
The quality evaluation of ANTARCTIC_OMI_SI_exten t is based on the “multi -product” 
approach as introduced in the second issue of the Ocean State Report (CMEMS OSR, 2017). 
Five global products have been used to build the ensemble mean, and its associated ensemble 
spread (Figure 1, dark red line and blue s haded area). Results of Fig. 1 indicate how different 
products compare to each other in view of their mean seasonal cycle and their interannual 
variability, e.g. annual means. 
 
 
Figure 1: A ntarctica sea ice extent of seasonal means (top panel) and annual mean (bottom panel). 
Units are in millions of km 2. Time series are based on different data products, i.e. global ocean 
reanalysis GLORYS (blue), C -GLORS (orange), ORAS5 (green), FOAM (purple) and the ensemble mean 
(red) and the ensemble spread (light blue). Details on the products are given in the corresponding PUM 
for this OMI.

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document evaluates the Ocean Monitoring Indicator for Antarctic Sea Ice Extent, detailing its methodology and validation process based on multiple global ocean reanalysis products.

variables.long_name: Sea ice extent defined as the area of ocean covered at least by 15% sea ice.
